lessons.title: Leave room for the part people remember.

image.alt: Folded paper, glass of water, pencil, and soft shadow

lessons.body: The fastest version is not always the smallest one. A little structure up front can make the creative part feel less fragile later, and it gives the weird, memorable part a better chance to survive.
lessons.credit: Shared by the house / edited for the issue

cards.label: Weekly ledger
cards.text: Birthdays, events, wins, and reminders live as one flexible editorial list.

items.label: Celebrate
items.text: Two birthdays and one quiet anniversary made the list.

items.label: Gather
items.text: The Thursday review window is the main shared moment this week.

items.label: Notice
items.text: A few strong posts from the feed deserve a second life in the issue.

items.label: Carry
items.text: The main lesson: leave enough room for the memorable thing to survive.

feature.title: Audrey is turning the Digest into a weekly ritual.
feature.body: Each issue can hold a headshot, supporting project images, and one short staff story, giving the house a repeatable format that still feels alive week to week.

images.alt: Supporting visual from the highlighted week

businessUnits.title: SH leads. The agencies add the texture.
businessUnits.lead: Start with the house. Let the lines travel outward.
businessUnits.note: This version leans hardest into SH as the primary read, then lets the agency breakdowns stay quick and scan-friendly.

supportingImage.alt: Layered paper and blueprint editorial still life

notes.text: Summit House is the anchor: one weekly read for what moved, mattered, and should travel.
Oust is shaping the campaign around the clearest story.
Ritual has the next production plan tightened for handoff.
Wild Places is getting the effects language into a usable shape.
Meridian is making the handoff layer feel quieter.
Big Bird brings the bright social signal into the same house rhythm.

sharing.title: Seen in the feed, saved for the house.
sharing.body: The posts, screenshots, launches, and bright little public signals that should make it back into the house conversation.

posts.title: A studio update is only useful if people can find it again.
